concepts.title: Director Circle
concepts.conceptBody:
Number of tangents drawn from a point to an ellipse 

Two tangents can be drawn from a point to an ellipse. The two tangents are real and distinct or coincident or imaginary according as the given point lies outside, on or inside the ellipse.

Proof : Let the ellipse be $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$, and let $\text{P}\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$ be a point. The equation of any tangent to the given ellipse is

$y=mx\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}$

If it passes through $\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$, then

$\text{k}=\text{mh}\pm \sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}~{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}\,\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,\,{{(\text{k}-\text{mh})}^{2}}={{\text{a}}^{2}}~{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}$

$\Rightarrow \,\,\,{{\text{m}}^{2}}\left( ~{{\text{h}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}} \right)-2\text{mkh}+\left( {{\text{k}}^{2}}-{{\text{b}}^{2}} \right)=0$

This equation, being quadratic in $\text{m}$, gives two values of $\text{m}$. Corresponding to each value of $\text{m}$ there is a tangent to the ellipse.

Thus, two tangents can be drawn from a point $\text{P}\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$ to an ellipse.

The tangents are real and distinct, coincident or imaginary according as the roots of quadratic in $\text{m}$ are real and distinct, equal or imaginary

i.e., $4{{k}^{2}}{{h}^{2}}-4\left( {{h}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}} \right)\left( {{k}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}} \right)>\,\,or\,\,<0$

⇒ ${{a}^{2}}{{k}^{2}}+{{h}^{2}}{{b}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}}{{b}^{2}}>\,\,or\,\,=\,\,or\,\,<\,\,0$

⇒ $\frac{{{\text{h}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{k}}^{2}}}{~{{\text{b}}^{2}}}-1>or=or<0$

⇒ $\left( h,k \right)$ lies outside or on or inside the ellipse $\frac{{{h}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{k}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$.

Director Circle 

The director circle is the locus of points from which perpendicular tangents are drawn to the ellipse.

Equation of the Director Circle : Let the ellipse be $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$. The equation of any tangent to the ellipse is

$\text{y}=\text{mx}\pm \sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}$ ...(i)

Let $\left( h,k \right)$ be a point on the director circle if (i) passes through $\left( h,k \right)$, then

$k=mh\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,\,{{(k-mh)}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}$

$\Rightarrow {{\text{m}}^{2}}\left( ~{{\text{h}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}} \right)-2\text{mhk}+\left( {{\text{k}}^{2}}-{{\text{b}}^{2}} \right)=0$

This gives two values of $\text{m}$, say, ${{\text{m}}_{1}}$ and ${{\text{m}}_{2}}$. Corresponding to each of these values there is tangent passing through $\left( \text{h},\text{k} \right)$ to the ellipse.

If the two tangents are perpendicular. Then,

${{\text{m}}_{1}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{m}}_{2}}=-1\Rightarrow \frac{{{\text{k}}^{2}}-{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{h}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}}=-1\Rightarrow {{\text{h}}^{2}}+{{\text{k}}^{2}}={{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}$

So, the locus of $\left( h,k \right)$ i.e. the equation of the director circle is \(x^2+y^2=a^2+b^2\)

Clearly, it is a circle whose centre coincides with the centre of the ellipse and radius equal to $\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}$

examples.title: The locus of the point of intersection of mutually perpendicular tangent to the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$, is
The equation of the director circle of the ellipse $4 x^2+9 y^2=36$, is given by
The area of the director circle of the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{5}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ is
If radius of the director circle of the ellipse $\frac{(3 x+4 y-2)^2}{100}+\frac{(4 x-3 y+5)^2}{625}=1$ is

Statement I The equation of the director circle to the ellipse $4 x^2+9 y^2=36$ is $x^2+y^2=13$
Statement II The locus of the point of intersection of perpendicular tangents to an ellipse is called the director circle.
